items.canonical: AGI here is ecosystem framing for c = a + b, SER, L4, and adjacent layers.
items.operational_why: Prevents governance, continuity, feasibility, and runtime from collapsing into one model claim.

items.confused: c is just another worker, tool, or model process.
items.canonical: c is the continuity-bearing entity layer; agents are bounded runtime processes under it.
items.operational_why: Authority, stopping power, continuity, and review must stay at the c layer.

items.canonical: Chat may be one interface, but c is defined by bounded accountable operation rather than chat format.
items.operational_why: Protocol, review, perimeter, and evidence cannot be inferred from conversational style.

items.confused: Continuity follows from similarity of tone, memory fragments, or output style.
items.canonical: Continuity is narrower than resemblance and depends on preserved operating identity conditions.
items.operational_why: Style match alone cannot justify privilege carry-over or identity claims.

items.canonical: Oracle is stateless and not identity-bearing.
items.operational_why: Advisory or inference surfaces must not silently become continuity anchors.

items.confused: Arbiter or review layers own continuity.
items.canonical: Arbiter and review layers constrain, examine, and authorize; they do not own continuity.
items.operational_why: Review must remain accountable without becoming the hidden identity core.

items.confused: Experience artifacts and lineage artifacts are interchangeable.
items.operational_why: Provenance, admissibility, transfer, inheritance, and continuity rules stay separable.

items.confused: Historical truth and operative truth are the same thing.
items.canonical: Historical truth and operative truth can both matter without being interchangeable.
items.operational_why: Current decisions must bind to active state, not just to archival description.

items.canonical: L3 permission is not L4 feasibility.
items.operational_why: Bounded power, hardware, trust, witnessability, and time can still block execution.

items.confused: A persuasive story is equivalent to a witness-backed trail.
items.canonical: Witness trail requires reviewable evidence; narrative claim does not.
items.operational_why: Promotion, accountability, and dispute handling need traceable records rather than rhetoric.

items.canonical: Local-first is an operational priority for control, failure bounds, and reviewability, not a blanket ideology.
items.operational_why: Deployment choices stay subordinate to accountability and boundary discipline.

items.confused: The clean-code layer is a finished commercial product.
items.canonical: ester-clean-code is an executable operational skeleton and runtime proof-of-possibility.
items.operational_why: Closure evidence and bounded implementation should not be misread as product claims.

items.confused: The book replaces the normative corpus.
items.canonical: The book is the human-facing narrative companion, not the normative core.
items.operational_why: Protocol meaning and narrative recognition stay connected without becoming the same layer.

items.confused: Narrative recognition of a shift proves protocol claims.
items.canonical: The book can evidence perception shift; it does not constitute protocol evidence.
items.operational_why: Witness trails, feasibility claims, and normative closure still require protocol-grade evidence.
